signatures.description: Uses cmstp.exe sendkeys technique to bypass User Access Control (UAC)

signatures.name: unbacked_api_resolution
signatures.description: Manually resolves API addresses from dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory, indicative of shellcode or an unpacker

signatures.name: unbacked_bind_shell
signatures.description: Bound a network socket to listen for inbound connections from dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory, indicating a fileless TCP bind shell or P2P

signatures.name: unbacked_com_instantiation
signatures.description: Attempted to use a COM object (CoCreateInstance) from dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory, possibly for WMI reconnaissance or DCOM lateral movement

signatures.name: unbacked_crypto_operations
signatures.description: Invoked native Windows cryptographic APIs from dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory, possible encryption/decryption of payloads, c2, files or data

signatures.name: unbacked_delay_execution
signatures.description: Paused execution (sleep/delay) in a thread executing in dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory, indicative of sandbox evasion or C2 sleeping between callbacks

signatures.name: unbacked_dns_resolution
signatures.description: Attempted to resolve a domain name from dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory

signatures.name: unbacked_dotnet_execution
signatures.description: Attempted to load .NET DLLs or call CLR APIs from dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory, indicative of fileless .NET

signatures.name: unbacked_exception_filter
signatures.description: Modified exception handling mechanisms (UEF/VEH) from dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory, indicative of fileless anti-debugging or silent crash suppression

signatures.name: unbacked_file_dropping
signatures.description: Wrote data to the filesystem from dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory

signatures.name: unbacked_library_load
signatures.description: Loads a new DLL where the caller address originates from dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory

signatures.name: unbacked_memory_apc_execution
signatures.description: Queues an Asynchronous Procedure Call (APC) where the routine points to dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory, possibly to execute shellcode

signatures.name: unbacked_memory_network_connection
signatures.description: Network connection initiated from dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory, indicative of fileless C2 activity

signatures.name: unbacked_memory_protection_alteration
signatures.description: Altered memory protections from dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory, indicative of self-modifying shellcode or memory patching

signatures.name: unbacked_mutex_creation
signatures.description: Created or queried a mutex from dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory, indicative of a fileless payload checking or creating an infection marker

signatures.name: unbacked_named_pipe_creation
signatures.description: Attempted to create a named pipe from dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory, possibly indicative of fileless Peer-to-Peer (P2P) SMB beacon

signatures.name: unbacked_process_creation
signatures.description: Attempted to spawn a new child process from dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory

signatures.name: unbacked_process_enumeration
signatures.description: Attempted to enumerate running processes from dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory

signatures.name: unbacked_process_mitigation_alteration
signatures.description: Manipulated process mitigation policies (CFG/DEP/hard error modes) from dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory

signatures.name: unbacked_registry_modification
signatures.description: Attempted to modify the Windows registry from dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory

signatures.name: unbacked_service_manipulation
signatures.description: Attempted to interact with the Service Control Manager from dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory

signatures.name: unbacked_syscall_execution
signatures.description: Executes syscalls where the return address or caller points to dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory

signatures.name: unbacked_token_manipulation
signatures.description: Attempted to open, duplicate, or impersonate an access token from dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory, indicative credential theft or lateral movement

signatures.name: unbacked_useragent_retrieval
signatures.description: Queried the system's user-agent string from dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory, likely to use in C2 to avoid a hardcoded user-agent

signatures.name: unbacked_veh_registration
signatures.description: Registers a Vectored Exception Handler (VEH) where the callback function points to unbacked memory, indicating hardware breakpoint hooking or obfuscation

signatures.name: unbacked_wmi_execution
signatures.description: Invoked a WMI method from a thread executing in dynamically allocated (unbacked) memory
